--- Thread (12 messages, 2020-09-06) ---
[2020-09-06 01:54] elyot: I think in the past, we never thought that the "default" openings were so special in terms of how they shaped that game, probably because we were constantly changing parts of the base set and openings were always in flux
[2020-09-06 01:55] elyot: So I don't think it ever occurred to us that gold handicaps were bad because they changed openings
[2020-09-06 01:55] elyot: like to us it almost didn't matter because openings in some way changed every game with new units
[2020-09-06 01:56] elyot: and I think for newer players, the same thing is true in that they do not get too attached to openings or develop a deep understanding of like, how things tend to go in certain sets, they tend to feel it out each game individually
[2020-09-06 01:56] elyot: so I'm not sure that having extra gold is a real impediment to them learning the game, because I think learning specific openings is really not so important for beginners
[2020-09-06 01:58] elyot: It would not be hard to add other handicaps like a long-delay gauss cannon but those are tricky in that their value can be different if the set is fast or slow, and they can also pollute the sense of what type of strat is good in what situation so I think it's just substituting one kind of bias/problem for another
[2020-09-06 01:59] .bky_1556 (bky): I chose long-delay gauss cannons as my example because I think their value is less variable.
[2020-09-06 02:01] .bky_1556 (bky): Less variable than handicap gold, even, because sometimes you want to do your P2 natural conduit even though you have handicap gold,
[2020-09-06 02:01] .bky_1556 (bky): and then sometimes the handicap gold is really good precisely because it lets you more naturally skip that conduit.
[2020-09-06 02:02] .bky_1556 (bky): The gauss cannon, on the other hand...
[2020-09-06 02:03] .bky_1556 (bky): It's rare that you both don't want any attack by turn six, and don't inconvenience the opponent by using the 'free' poke to make them buy an absorber earlier,
[2020-09-06 02:05] .bky_1556 (bky): and also fairly rare that the gauss cannon mode of attack is inappropriate. Or, at least, nearly as inappropriate as slotting a tarsier into your breachpoof strat.

[2020-09-06 16:51] anesotericman (Esoteric): So I just, whether or not handicapping of some kind, what else would help the transition? [2020-09-06 16:52] anesotericman (Esoteric): What are people's current feelings on events? That's where some of the biggest skill gaps between the two players can happen, but also where you don't really need to protect the integrity of the game so much
[2020-09-06 16:53] anesotericman (Esoteric): I was just typing what I was thinking in my head as I was thinking it.
[2020-09-06 16:54] silentslayers: Events seem to encourage new players a bit
[2020-09-06 16:54] silentslayers: since i see lots of tier 1s there that go to them
[2020-09-06 16:54] willflame: personally i don't really like events because i feel like i don't know how to deal with the changes whereas the other people i'm playing do
[2020-09-06 16:55] willflame: i guess it reminds me of being a beginner again 😅
[2020-09-06 16:58] anesotericman (Esoteric): Most of the single player content doesn't have base set, and many events modify it to the point that normal build orders don't apply. Just makes me wonder how much of prismata pro player knowledge is locked into the base+X no gold handicap multiplayer configuration, and if that part is the barrier more than anything
[2020-09-06 16:59] anesotericman (Esoteric): but yeah, depends on how much of a given player's knowledge is in build orders vs unit characterists and game math
[2020-09-06 16:59] silentslayers: well ofc we have barely any incentive to do anything other than focus on base+x no gold handicap multiplayer configuration. If there were other incentives people would study those other stuff too
[2020-09-06 16:59] silentslayers: no one studies event weird stuff
[2020-09-06 16:59] silentslayers: but yeah it sucks for new players going against ppl who know tons of builds
[2020-09-06 17:00] silentslayers: they just gotta get owned i guess
[2020-09-06 17:00] silentslayers: ideally they have new ppl to play against tho
[2020-09-06 17:00] anesotericman (Esoteric): yeah, but barring that, what are some options
[2020-09-06 17:01] silentslayers: are these hypothetical suggestions that elyots never going to implement
[2020-09-06 17:01] silentslayers: or do you want something the community can do
[2020-09-06 17:02] anesotericman (Esoteric): just spitballing, but would any of these help? More distinct AI's, at slightly higher skill (definitely possible to implement by Elyot) More focus on events or other ways to make more players have even matches with newer players (depends on the way) more visibility for match finding so 3 new players, one in casual, one in ranked, and one in event can find each other.
[2020-09-06 17:02] willflame: as someone with ladder anxiety, i didn't have a problem playing against bots to improve
[2020-09-06 17:02] silentslayers: I mean probably but those are things elyot wont do
